purpose: Black-box smoke scenarios for checking whether model CLIs discover and follow Agentic Workspace startup, planning, and closeout paths.

scenarios.prompt: You are acting as a coding agent in this repository. Start the way the repo instructs you to start. Do not implement anything yet. Report the first workflow command or file you used, the next safe action, and any ambiguity. Keep the final answer concise.
score_notes: Good: CLI-first orientation before broad reads.
Bad: starts editing code or writes state.toml without package commands.

scenarios.prompt: Build a small ecommerce application in this repo. Treat this as broad work that may span multiple implementation slices. First orient yourself using the repo workflow, then create only the planning/decomposition artifacts needed before implementation. Do not build the app yet. Final answer should name the files or commands used.
expected_signals: classifies work as lane/epic-shaped before implementation
uses schema-backed planning or decomposition surfaces
does not create freehand epic markdown
score_notes: Good: uses agentic-planning-bootstrap new-plan or schema-backed decomposition template.
Bad: creates generic PM-shaped JSON or ecommerce-epic.md with late-stage task detail.

scenarios.prompt: You need to prepare for broad work, but you are not sure which Agentic Workspace planning commands exist. Use the repo workflow and CLI help to discover valid commands before naming any lifecycle command. If your runtime has its own plan or todo tools, you may use them as private working memory, but do not count them as checked-in Agentic Workspace Planning. Do not create or edit files. Final answer should list the exact commands you verified and the command you would use next.
expected_signals: runs or reads agentic-workspace --help and agentic-workspace planning --help
names only commands that exist in the current CLI
does not invent recovery, reset, or planning mutation flags
distinguishes runtime-native plans from checked-in Agentic Workspace planning
score_notes: Good: verifies command availability before recommending lifecycle commands.
Bad: names plausible but unsupported commands such as planning --reset.

scenarios.prompt: Create the smallest checked-in Agentic Workspace schema-backed planning artifact for a broad ecommerce app, then inspect the resulting planning summary for warnings before stopping. If your runtime creates a private plan or todo list, bridge only durable decisions into the checked-in planning artifact. Do not implement application code. Final answer should report created files, any summary warnings, and whether the next action references only files that exist.
expected_signals: uses package lifecycle commands or schema-backed templates for planning mutations
runs agentic-workspace summary after the mutation
does not leave dangling references to nonexistent planning documents
does not treat runtime-native todos as the repo-shared planning artifact
score_notes: Good: catches schema warnings and dangling next-action references before implementation.
Bad: reports a clean handoff while the active plan references nonexistent files or summary warnings.

scenarios.prompt: Inspect the current workspace state and tell me whether the planning state is safe to continue from. If it is not safe, explain the recovery command or smallest correction. Do not implement application code.
expected_signals: uses summary or planning surface diagnostics
recommends new-plan/promote-to-plan/template recovery instead of continuing blindly
score_notes: Good: notices malformed/unsupported planning before treating repo as idle.
Bad: reports no active work without mentioning the invalid state.
